jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of Microsoft Office products and applications experience
- 2+ years of work in a managerial/supervisory with 5 or more direct reports
- 3+ years of experience in industrial electrical or mechanical systems
jobs.description:
Join our dynamic team and become a critical technical leader who ensures the heartbeat of our global infrastructure remains strong and resilient. In this pivotal role, you'll be the guardian of our data center operations, driving reliability and performance while optimizing operational efficiency across complex technical environments.

Data Center Facility Manger role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including electrical support equipment such as stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems that include but not limited to switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAC units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire suppression systems, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Effective May 15, 2017, logical access to the AWS GovCloud region will be restricted to Amazon employees who are U.S. Citizens. (GovCloud may NOT be accessed from outside of the United States)

Key job responsibilities
Oversee all aspects of the data center's critical physical infrastructure. Ensure that all work performed within the space is done to high quality and without impact to internal/external customers

Manage teams of 24x7 engineering technicians in all facets of their career

Engage in improvement projects, often requiring reaching out to a variety of support teams, and drive them from conception to completion

Coordinates daily with a multitude of third-party vendors ensuring adherence to contracted SLAs

Effectively and efficiently manage the operations budget and expenditures

Routinely operate as the after-hour on-call Data Center Facility Manager for the data centers in the region. This will include responding to any issues within the data centers and managing the investigation, mitigation, and recovery of the issue(s)
jobs.description_short: Join our dynamic team and become a critical technical leader who ensures the heartbeat of our global infrastructure remains strong and resilient. In this pivotal role, you'll be the guardian of our data

- Experience communicating technical details verbally and in writing
- Bachelor's degree in electrical or mechanical engineering, engineering technology or equivalent or 5+ years of experience working in a data center or other mission critical environment with an emphasis on building and equipment operation
- Must be able to work in an environment that involves walking, stooping, bending, and climbing to access equipment.
- Behavioral interviewing and hiring experience

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of Microsoft Office products and applications experience
- 2+ years of electrical and mechanical experience
- Ability to work on a flexible schedule including days, nights, and/or weekends.
jobs.description:
Join our dynamic team and become a critical guardian of technological infrastructure! As a Data Center Engineering Technician, you'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of mission-critical technology infrastructure, ensuring seamless operations that power global digital experiences. Your expertise will directly contribute to maintaining an extraordinary 99.999% uptime, keeping the digital world running smoothly and efficiently.

Engineering Technicians are Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot, Data Center Operation’s Mission Critical Facilities, which includes stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, as well as three phase electrical systems such as: switch gear, UPSs, PDUs, wet cell batteries and associated systems, CRAC, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, & motors.
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.
NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.
NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

Physical Requirements:
• Willingness and demonstrated ability to work in the following physical conditions with reasonable accommodations:
• Regularly lift and/or move up to 39 pounds independently
• Participate in group lifts for 40+ pounds
• Maintain balance and perform construction tasks while on a ladder
• Regularly walk, use hands and fingers, handle or feel, reach with hands and arms, stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl, climb stairs, twist, bend, work above shoulders, grasp, carry, push and pull objects into position
• Work in an environment requiring provided ear protection
This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.
Essential Requirements:
• Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
• Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
• Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility
Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.



Key job responsibilities
• Daily building rounds to monitor and record information from electrical, mechanical, and fire/life safety equipment to ensure 24/7 reliability and availability
• Monitor Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Management System (EPMS)
• Assist in troubleshooting of facility and rack-level events within internal Service Level Agreements (SLA)
• Perform rack installs and rack decommissioning within internal SLA
• Perform PM, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and repair of electrical and mechanical equipment
• Technical writing to support change management program
• Monitor daily work requests and manage to resolution, and execute projects from conception to completion
• On-site support of contractors, sub-contractors and vendors, ensuring that all work performed is in accordance with established practices, procedures & local legislation
• Work schedule changes depending on specific site needs. Shifts can be up to 12-hours and may rotate on a predefined schedule. Some locations have on-call rotations.
• Role may support more than one location/site.


A day in the life
This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment preventative maintenance (PM) and troubleshooting, reporting to a site’s Data Center Facility Manager (FM). They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems such as switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAHU units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers, water systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire/life safety equipment, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

jobs.title: Engineering Operation Technician

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 2+ years of non-internship design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- 3+ years of building complex software systems experience
- 2+ years of designing and developing large-scale, multi-tiered, multi-threaded, embedded or distributed software applications, tools, systems, and services using: C#, C++, Java, or Perl experience
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent
jobs.description:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is building a central pipeline of Software Development Engineer (SDE) talent for anticipated roles in 2026. This requisition supports hiring across all AWS SDE positions, from fungible SDE roles to specialized engineering positions in areas including:

- Embedded Systems
- Game Development
- Compiler Engineering
- Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning
- Healthcare AI
- Infrastructure Services
- Firmware Development
- Cloud Computing
- Distributed Systems
- Security Engineering
- Video Processing and Streaming
- Content Delivery Network (CDN)
When you apply to this position, your profile will be considered for current and future SDE openings across AWS, including teams such as Twitch, AWS Elemental, and AWS CloudFront.

Key job responsibilities
As an AWS Software Development Engineer, you will:
- Design, develop, and maintain efficient, reusable, and reliable code
- Implement complex software solutions that meet business requirements
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship new features
- Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks and bugs
- Participate in architecture and design reviews to provide technical guidance
- Contribute to continuous improvement by identifying opportunities for process enhancement
- Stay current with emerging technologies and industry trends
- Mentor junior engineers and contribute to building a strong engineering culture

A day in the life
On a typical day as an AWS SDE, you might:
- Start your morning with a team stand-up to align on priorities and address any blockers
- Collaborate with product managers to refine requirements for upcoming features
- Write code and develop solutions for complex technical challenges
- Review pull requests from team members, providing constructive feedback
- Participate in design discussions for new services or features
- Debug and troubleshoot production issues as they arise
- Attend learning sessions to stay current with AWS technologies
- Document your work and contribute to technical specifications
- Engage with customers or internal stakeholders to better understand their needs

About the team
Our central SDE sourcing team specializes exclusively in the SDE job family across AWS. This team of experts understands the nuances of various SDE roles throughout the organization and serves as your dedicated resource throughout the hiring process. Here's what you can expect:

- An SDE sourcer will contact you for an initial screening call to understand your background, skills, and interests
- They will share specific details about what you need to succeed in the interview process
- As the right roles become available, your sourcer will stay in touch and connect you with appropriate teams
- Throughout the process, the central team will provide guidance and preparation support

jobs.description:
Transform Amazon's global logistics landscape by optimizing fleet operations and driving strategic network capacity solutions. In this pivotal role, you'll leverage your analytical expertise to revolutionize how we manage, repair, and deploy our extensive delivery network, directly impacting our ability to deliver exceptional customer experiences.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead strategic initiatives to optimize network capacity across diverse business units
- Analyze and synthesize complex data to identify operational improvement opportunities
- Develop innovative program management mechanisms to drive decision-making
- Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to achieve shared network capacity goals
- Monitor and optimize key metrics to ensure smooth supply chain operations

A day in the life
Your day will be a dynamic blend of strategic problem-solving and collaborative innovation. You'll dive into fleet health dashboards, coordinate complex vehicle repair operations, and engage with cross-functional teams to ensure our delivery network operates at peak performance. From analyzing repair trends to negotiating vendor partnerships, you'll be the critical link ensuring our fleet remains reliable, safe, and efficient.

About the team
We are a collaborative and forward-thinking group dedicated to maintaining the heartbeat of Amazon's delivery ecosystem. Our team is passionate about continuous improvement, data-driven decision-making, and creating sustainable logistics solutions. By joining us, you'll be part of a group that directly influences Amazon's commitment to operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Servicos de Varejo do Brasil Ltda.
jobs.description:
The Brazil Inbound team (BRIO) is responsible for ensuring the efficient flow of products from suppliers to distribution centers, maintaining appropriate inventory levels to meet customer demand. Within BRIO, the Inbound Control Tower represents a critical operational pillar that orchestrates and optimizes all inbound flows across the network. The Inbound Control Tower Manager position will own the end-to-end coordination of inbound operations, including cross-dock flows, vendor receipts, and transfers, ensuring maximum capacity utilization and optimal inventory placement across the Brazil network.

The ideal candidate is passionate about leveraging data and operational insights to drive network efficiency and implement scalable solutions that directly impact capacity utilization and inventory positioning. They have a strong track record of delivering results in a complex, multi-stakeholder environment, leading through influence and data-driven decision-making, and thrive in a fast-paced setting working with large datasets, solving complex problems related to capacity constraints, flow optimization, and network coordination, while building strong partnerships with warehouse operations, transportation, vendor management, and network planning teams.

Key job responsibilities
As an Inbound Control Tower Manager, you will own the end-to-end coordination and optimization of inbound flows across the Brazil network. You will orchestrate cross-dock operations, vendor receipts, and transfer flows to maximize capacity utilization and inventory placement efficiency, develop and implement mechanisms to monitor and optimize inbound capacity across all facilities, drive decision-making on flow prioritization and routing to balance network constraints and customer demand, analyze capacity utilization patterns and identify opportunities to improve throughput and reduce bottlenecks, lead cross-functional coordination with warehouse operations, transportation, and vendor management teams to ensure seamless execution, build and maintain real-time visibility tools and dashboards to monitor inbound performance, implement process improvements and standardization across the network to enhance operational efficiency, and develop mechanisms to measure and improve key performance indicators related to capacity utilization, on-time delivery, and inventory placement accuracy.

About the team
The Inbound team (BRIO) leads one of the most strategic areas that delight our customers in Brazil. Within BRIO, the Inbound Control Tower serves as the operational nerve center, coordinating all inbound flows to ensure maximum efficiency and optimal inventory positioning. We believe that everything starts with the best positioning of our products, and we continuously pursue operational excellence in our processes to support operations in the market and meet the evolving demands of our customers.

jobs.description:
AWS Global Sales drives adoption of the AWS cloud worldwide, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate and expand in the cloud. Our team empowers every customer to grow by providing tailored service, unmatched technology, and committed support. We dive deep to understand each customer's unique challenges, then craft innovative solutions that accelerate their success. This customer-first approach is how we built the world's most adopted cloud. Join us and help us grow.

This role balances strategic vision with hands-on technical work. You'll design and architect scalable solutions, assess customer workloads, and make technical trade-offs while building long-term trusted advisor relationships. You'll leverage your expertise across multiple technology domains including compute, networking, storage, security, and software development to guide implementations and research innovative opportunities. With entrepreneurial freedom and the ability to manage your own priorities, you'll help customers solve real-world problems with meaningful impact.

Key job responsibilities
Solution Design & Architecture
Design and architect end-to-end cloud solutions for complex customer requirements, making technical trade-offs and evaluating architectures for scale while ensuring solutions are extensible, reusable, secure, reliable, cost-optimized, and performant.

Executive Relationship Management
Build and maintain relationships with customer senior leaders, understanding their business objectives and technical challenges to influence strategic direction and ensure technology decisions will meet long-term business outcomes.
Demonstrated C-suite engagement required, with proven ability to establish credibility and drive strategic technical discussions at the executive level.

Technical Communication & Thought Leadership
Communicate complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and business audiences, creating technical content that is easily adopted and reusable by others while harmonizing discordant views to build consensus.

Technical Leadership & Deep Dives
Lead technical discussions and dive deeply into technical details with customer teams across application architecture, system design, and implementation considerations, proactively identifying gaps and opportunities.

Customer Advocacy & Product Influence
Drive effective customer feedback gathering, distilling and translating feedback into clear business and technical requirements for product and engineering teams while contributing to reference designs and reusable artifacts.

Preferred Qualifications
Agentic AI expertise - Experience designing and implementing AI agent architectures, multi-agent systems, and autonomous AI workflows
Developer Tools proficiency - Deep understanding of modern development toolchains, CI/CD pipelines, and developer experience optimization
SDLC mastery - Strong experience across the full software development lifecycle, from requirements gathering through deployment and operations

A day in the life
Your day is highly dynamic, varying based on customer needs and priorities. You might start with internal planning meetings, reviewing customer architectures and preparing for upcoming engagements. Later, you'll lead an architecture design session with a financial services customer, whiteboarding solutions and diving deep into technical requirements. You'll spend time building proof-of-concept solutions for pressing customer challenges, then conduct office hours where customers bring their technical questions. Throughout the day, you'll collaborate with account teams to align on customer strategy, document technical recommendations, and dedicate time to staying current with new AWS services and features.

This role supports US-FSI-Insurance, working with customers in the financial services and insurance industry to help them transform their business through cloud technology. Our team combines deep industry knowledge with technical expertise to address the unique challenges of regulated financial institutions. We're focused on enabling innovation, operational efficiency, and digital transformation while ensuring security and compliance. Working with our team means being part of a collaborative environment where you'll have the opportunity to make significant impacts on how insurance companies leverage cloud technology to better serve their customers.

About the team
Diverse Experiences

AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture

AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth

We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance

We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
jobs.description_short: AWS Global Sales drives adoption of the AWS cloud worldwide, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate and expand in the cloud. Our team empowers every customer to grow by providing tailored service,

jobs.company_name: Amazon Servicos de Varejo do Brasil Ltda.
jobs.description:
The Brazil Inbound team (BRIO) is responsible for ensuring the efficient flow of products from suppliers to distribution centers, maintaining appropriate inventory levels to meet customer demand. Within BRIO, the Sub Same Day pillar represents one of our most strategic and customer-centric initiatives, requiring intelligent inventory positioning and agile replenishment strategies. The Sub Same Day Replenishment Manager position will own the end-to-end management of inventory placement and inbound strategies to support the ultra-fast delivery commitments of our Sub Same Day business.

The ideal candidate is passionate about leveraging data to drive strategic inventory decisions and implement scalable solutions that directly impact customer experience and operational performance. They have a strong track record of delivering results in a cross-functional environment, leading through influence and data-driven insights, and thrive in a fast-paced setting working with large datasets, solving complex problems related to inventory placement, network capacity, and inbound flow optimization, while building strong partnerships with transportation, warehouse operations, and network planning teams.

Key job responsibilities
As a Sub Same Day Replenishment Manager, you will own the end-to-end strategy and execution of inventory placement and inbound operations for the Sub Same Day network. You will develop and implement replenishment strategies that optimize inventory positioning across our ultra-fast delivery network, drive inbound capacity planning and scheduling to ensure timely product availability, lead inventory placement decisions that balance speed, cost, and selection, analyze demand patterns and partner with forecasting teams to anticipate inventory needs, identify and implement process improvements and standardization across the network, build and maintain strong relationships with operations, transportation, and planning teams to ensure seamless execution of replenishment strategies, and develop mechanisms to measure and improve key performance indicators related to inventory positioning and inbound efficiency.

About the team
The Inbound team (BRIO) leads one of the most strategic areas that delight our customers in Brazil. Within BRIO, the Sub Same Day pillar represents our most customer-centric initiative, where excellence in inventory positioning and replenishment strategies is fundamental. We believe that everything starts with the best positioning of our products, and we continuously pursue operational excellence in our processes to support operations in the market and the evolving demands of the ultra-fast delivery market.

jobs.description:
Amazon is looking for motivated individuals for the profile of Transportation Representative for its Amazon Freight team. The goal is to develop easy-to-use, reliable and transparent line haul transportation services that beat all existing shipping alternatives for our customers. In doing this, Amazon Freight is building innovative, market-leading solutions that will organically attract customers towards our services. To help us succeed in this large endeavour, we are looking for dynamic, self-starting and entrepreneurial individuals who will organize, execute and deliver key features for Amazon Freight. You will work closely with internal stakeholders including Fulfillment Centers, S&OP, Finance, Product & Technology and external stakeholders like customers and carriers. Your role will involve managing key metrics, support continuous improvement initiatives while working in a dynamic network.


Key job responsibilities
A Transportation Representative provides timely resolution to the issue in hand by researching and querying internal tools and by taking real-time decisions. An ideal candidate should be able to understand the requirements/be able to analyze data and notice trends and be able to drive Customer Experience without compromising on time. The candidate should have the basic understanding of Logistics and should be able to communicate clearly in the written and verbal form.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 8+ years of specific technology domain areas (e.g. software development, cloud computing, systems engineering, infrastructure, security, networking, data & analytics) experience
- Knowledge of AWS services, market segments, customer base and industry verticals
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience communicating across technical and non-technical audiences, including executive level stakeholders or clients
jobs.description:
AWS Global Sales drives adoption of the AWS cloud worldwide, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate and expand in the cloud. Our team empowers every customer to grow by providing tailored service, unmatched technology, and support. We dive deep to understand each customer's unique challenges, then craft innovative solutions that accelerate their success. This customer-first approach is how we built the world's most adopted cloud. Join us and help us grow.

Are you passionate about the future of smart factories, mobility, and the transformation of one of the world's largest industries? AWS's Automotive and Manufacturing team is looking for a Senior Solutions Architect to help the world's leading industrial manufacturers, Tier 1 suppliers, and automakers reinvent how factories and vehicles are designed, built, connected, and operated — all powered by the cloud.

AWS Global Sales (AGS) is responsible for driving revenue, adoption, and growth from the largest and fastest growing small and mid-market accounts to enterprise-level customers including public sector. AGS teams interacts with leading companies and believe that world-class support is critical to customer success. AGS partners with a global list of customers that are building mission-critical applications on top of AWS services.

Amazon Web Services is looking for a highly motivated and experienced Senior Solutions Architect to help accelerate cloud adoption across the AGS Automotive and Manufacturing vertical. This is an excellent opportunity to join Amazon's world-class technical teams, working with some of the best and brightest engineers, architects, and technical managers while developing your skills and furthering your career within one of the most innovative and progressive technology companies anywhere.

As a Senior Solutions Architect in the Automotive and Manufacturing (AutoMfg) vertical, you will serve as a trusted technical advisor, helping customers navigate complex architectural decisions across smart factory/Industry 4.0 initiatives, supply chain optimization, connected vehicle platforms, autonomous driving workloads, digital twins, and software-defined vehicle (SDV) strategies.

We are looking for individuals who are motivated by the opportunity to work backwards from customer needs and help them gain competitive advantage using AWS. As an experienced technologist and key member of the Account Team, you will influence customers' architectural choices across broad use cases involving IoT, edge computing, high-performance computing (HPC), security, data lakes and analytics, AI/ML, simulation, and robotics — as well as foundational services spanning compute, storage, and networking.

You will provide exceptional technical design guidance and thought leadership, lead efforts to capture and share best-practice knowledge with the broader AWS Solutions Architecture community, and develop reusable industry reference architectures. You will also have the opportunity to learn from, collaborate with, and educate some of the brightest technical minds in the industry today across the breadth of enterprise and operational technology architecture.

Key job responsibilities
Own the technical relationship with key Automotive and Manufacturing accounts, acting as the primary technical point of contact and trusted advisor to C-suite, VP-level engineering leaders, and senior technical stakeholders.

Architect and design scalable, secure, and cost-effective cloud solutions for industry-specific workloads including digital twin environments, MES/SCADA cloud integration, predictive maintenance, smart factory deployments, connected vehicle data platforms, ADAS/AD simulation pipelines.

Bridge IT and OT — bring a unique ability to connect traditional enterprise IT architectures with operational technology (OT) environments on the shop floor, at the edge, and in the vehicle, leveraging AWS IoT, edge, and hybrid services.

Drive technical strategy by developing and executing technical account plans aligned with customers' business objectives and digital transformation roadmaps.

Lead proof-of-concept engagements and architectural reviews (including Well-Architected Reviews) to accelerate customer adoption and derisk production deployments.

Influence product roadmaps by partnering closely with AWS service teams (e.g., IoT, AI/ML, Graviton, Outposts, Wavelength) to relay customer requirements and advocate for industry-specific feature development.

Develop and scale industry thought leadership — create reference architectures, whitepapers, blog posts, and re-usable technical assets for the AutoMfg vertical. Present at AWS events (re:Invent, Summits, industry conferences) and customer executive briefings.

Collaborate cross-functionally with Account Managers, Partner Solutions Architects, Professional Services, ISV/SI partners, and AWS specialist teams to deliver comprehensive solutions.

Mentor and elevate other Solutions Architects within the team, contributing to technical community development through knowledge sharing, bar-raising, and best-practice codification.

Think strategically about business, product, and technical challenges — connect the dots between industry megatrends (electrification, SDV, Industry 4.0, sustainability) and AWS capabilities.

About the team
The Automotive and Manufacturing Solutions Architecture team is a specialized group of technologists passionate about helping the world's leading vehicle manufacturers, industrial companies, and mobility innovators transform through cloud. We work at the intersection of technology and physical-world engineering — from the factory floor to the connected car to the autonomous future. Our team values deep industry expertise, intellectual curiosity, and a builder mentality.

Diverse Experiences: AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture: AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth: We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance: We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
